public_cache_policy.notes: A future public cache may store already-public metadata separately from private/user-selected data, but no cache runtime exists.

private_cache_policy.notes: Private cache is not implemented. Any future private cache requires explicit user choice, privacy review, and deletion/export controls.

local_path_policy.notes: Public-alpha status and static/public artifacts must not expose private local paths.

user_state_policy.notes: Future native preferences or strategy memory must remain local and explicit unless a later account/sync policy exists.

resolution_memory_policy.notes: Existing resolution-memory runtime remains local developer/reference behavior. Native private memory requires this policy and future implementation review.

telemetry_policy.notes: No telemetry, analytics, diagnostics upload, or crash-report upload exists in v0.

logging_policy.notes: Logs are not telemetry. Future native logs need retention, redaction, and user controls.

diagnostics_policy.notes: Diagnostics and crash reports are future and must be explicit user exports, not automatic uploads.

credential_policy.notes: No credential store exists. Future source credentials require a separate auth/secrets policy.

deletion_reset_policy.notes: Deletion/reset controls are required before any native private cache implementation.

export_policy.notes: Future exports must be user-initiated and distinguish public metadata from private state.

portable_mode_policy.notes: Portable/offline mode is future; it must keep cache roots explicit and resettable.

relay_policy.notes: Relay surfaces remain public/read-only by default and must not expose private cache, credentials, user history, or write/admin controls to old clients.

snapshot_policy.notes: Static snapshots remain public/offline artifacts and must not contain private cache or user state.

native_client_requirements: Native clients must keep private cache, telemetry, accounts, cloud sync, and private file ingestion disabled by default.
Native clients must separate public cache from private cache.
Native clients must require user-selected cache roots before writing private state.
Native clients must provide clear deletion, reset, and export controls before private cache implementation.
Native clients must avoid private local paths in public reports, snapshots, static pages, relay views, and support artifacts.
Native clients must not upload logs, diagnostics, crash reports, private inventories, or source credentials unless a future explicit opt-in policy exists.
notes: Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 is policy, contract, validation, and documentation only.
No cache runtime, private file ingestion, local archive scanning, telemetry, accounts, cloud sync, uploads, native client, relay runtime, or GUI behavior is implemented.
The default posture is privacy-first: local/private features are off by default and public artifacts must not leak private paths or secrets.

Relay Prototype Planning v0 keeps the first future relay prototype public, read-only, localhost-only by default, and static; no private cache roots, arbitrary user directories, credentials, telemetry, uploads, private paths, or private data are allowed.
This policy gates future native cache, relay private-data handling, diagnostics, portable mode, and local/offline client work.
